segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Short scout, right and left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, flipping a dagger and catching it by the spine.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "We don't pick fights, we don't collect curses, we come back before Thornik starts making spreadsheets."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Tables,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik corrected.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Spreadsheets are a myth from the west."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Of course they are,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex deadpanned, already moving.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Thornik, you're with me. If something needs unscrewing, you can talk to it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They took the eastern service corridor first -- a low-ceilinged gallery, the stone so smooth it had forgotten it had ever been rough. The smell there was stranger: faint metal sweetness, like the air over a well-kept blade; a hum underfoot as if the rock carried a memory of current.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Residual load,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik murmured, palm flat to the wall.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Conduction lines. The Hall leeches a steady trickle from micro-wards embedded in the bedrock. Old dwarven trick to keep structural fields topped."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He tapped the stone with a knuckle; the tone came back pure.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "This gallery is stable."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex crouched at a junction where the corridor branched, sniffed the air like a hunting dog, and laid a palm against each wall in turn.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Left smells of water. Right smells of nothing, which is worse."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The nothing-smell is old vacuum,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, consulting the journal.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Sealed section. Grandfather marked it with a rune I've only seen once before -- the one that means don't."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He adjusted his goggles.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Not 'danger.' Not 'caution.' Just don't."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Charming family tradition."

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "We have a lot of those. Most of them involve alcohol and regret."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They backed out and tried the western spur, which choked almost immediately: a mining tunnel collapsed and then fused, as if heat had melted its failure into permanence. The smell was glass and dust.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not our way,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex judged, crouching at the edge of a darkness that would not be argued with.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They returned to the threshold, to Serana's chalk mark watching the doorway like a lidded eye.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan's shadow moved first.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It slid a finger-length toward the sigil -- not with the light's logic, but with its own, pulled by something that didn't care about angles or sources.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The chalk lines brightened all at once, not gentle, not soft: a white-hot flare that hit the nose with scorched mineral and crisp ozone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shadow recoiled in a visible ripple, as if fire had run through oil -- fleeing back around Durgan's boots, seething there until he crushed it flat with effort that made his jaw jump.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nobody spoke.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The flare-light faded. The sigil settled back to its cold geometry. In the silence after, each of them found somewhere to look that wasn't Durgan's face -- the pillar carvings, the floor, the chalk line itself still faintly warm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The kind of silence that isn't absence of sound but absence of the right words.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik had been reaching for a device that no longer existed -- muscle memory, the instinct to measure -- and lowered his empty hand slowly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara studied the chalk marks with professional interest, her expression deliberately neutral, which was its own kind of commentary.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen pressed her fingertips together, feeling the vine-marks pulse their quiet alarm: wrongness, wrongness, still a person.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin watched Durgan's jaw and thought: He's not fighting us. He's fighting himself. And he's losing by inches.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan broke the silence. He wiped a single red thread from under his nostril with the back of his knuckle and looked at it as if surprised it existed. His jaw worked once, then settled.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "It will again,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Keep the marks fresh."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's chin lifted a degree.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "It noticed,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said -- not accusation, not pity. Accounting. She was already reaching for the chalk.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen touched the stone near the burned-bright sigil without crossing it, palm flat. The silver threads in her tattoos shivered.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "It hurt the wrongness without hating the person,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, a quiet relief in her voice.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I can feel the difference. We should lace the perimeter."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "On it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, already pocketing the stump of chalk and breaking a fresh stick.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Corners and sightlines. Nine strokes each. We keep the geometry honest."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara drifted parallel to Caelin as he cooled the buckle he'd mended.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Your restraint is very tiresome,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she observed, eyes on the faint steam.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Men half your age would already have tried to set the ceiling on fire to prove a point."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I only just learned how much it costs,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said. He flexed his fingers, testing the lattice ache.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I can't afford to show off."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's smile acknowledged the answer and then filed it in a drawer marked useful. She tucked her sleeves lower. If anyone noticed the way she favored her hands, they had the courtesy not to say.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They ringed the Hall with chalk, nine-stroke sigils at each throat where a passage met the open space.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The symbols gathered the lichen-light to themselves until the Deep Hall looked like a star-chart: pale constellations behind pillars and the slow, steady coal-glow of Caelin's scale. The air tasted faintly of dusty limestone and hot flint.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By late afternoon they had a map.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Three viable routes,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, tapping lines he'd sketched in charcoal on the central table.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Smithy run is flooded two at least two spans deep -- I can hear it breathing. Mining spur is fused at the choke. That leaves us the Forge Road with a vent shaft network as auxiliary escape."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Forge Road takes us toward the caldera?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Aye. Past it. There's a bridge and a long spiral stair to the east face. If it's not collapsed."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "And if it has?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex asked.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Then we improvise. Which, I should note, has been our entire strategy since Thornwick, and it's only nearly killed us four times."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Five,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I wasn't counting the scree slide."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Then five,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said from his pillar, and the fact that he'd been counting startled everyone except Vex, who nodded once in grim solidarity.
